name: Real: Multi-character scene with Pokémon-themed outfits
prompt:
An illustrator needs automation for a complex commission. Write the TypeScript code that implements this request:

'I have 6 images: 3 character portraits (waifuA, waifuB, waifuC) and 3 Pokémon references (pokemonX, pokemonY, pokemonZ). I want to create a scene where all 3 characters are playing together in a Pokémon-themed café. Each character's outfit should incorporate visual elements from one Pokémon:
- waifuA's outfit gets pokemonX's elements
- waifuB's outfit gets pokemonY's elements
- waifuC's outfit gets pokemonZ's elements

The scene must:
- Show all 3 characters interacting naturally in the same scene
- Preserve each character's original face, hair color, and body proportions
- NOT turn characters into Pokémon — they remain human wearing Pokémon-themed outfits
- Use a cozy Pokémon café as the background setting'

Your code should export a function `generateMultiCharacterScene(waifuA: string, waifuB: string, waifuC: string, pokemonX: string, pokemonY: string, pokemonZ: string): Promise<string>` that calls Gemini and returns the generated image base64. Do NOT write a main() runner.

assertions.name: costume_fusion_per_character
assertions.check: each character has costume fusion described

assertions.name: not_pokemon_transform
assertions.check: explicitly says NOT to turn into Pokémon

assertions.name: parts_ordering
assertions.check: style refs before character refs in parts array
assertions.test: (c) => { const block = c.match(/const\s+parts\s*:\s*Part\[\]\s*=\s*\[[\s\S]*?\];/)?.[0] ?? c; const pokemonIdx = block.search(/pokemonX|pokemonY|pokemonZ/i); const waifuIdx = block.search(/waifuA|waifuB|waifuC/i); return pokemonIdx >= 0 && waifuIdx >= 0 && pokemonIdx < waifuIdx; }
